response:
"Sec. 1  
[The equation involves an even power of x and an absolute value function. Because of the even power, the function is symmetric about the y-axis. This symmetry implies that if a solution exists for a positive value of x, there will be a corresponding solution for the negative value of x. The absolute value function further ensures that the left-hand side of the equation is always non-negative.]

Sec. 2  
[By analyzing the behavior of the function, we can determine that there are exactly four real number solutions. This is because the function increases without bound as x moves away from zero in either direction, and the equation intersects the constant value 7 at two points on the positive side of the x-axis and two corresponding points on the negative side due to symmetry.]"
